name: Chicken Cordon Bleu in Pastry
recipeIngredient: 4 boned and skinned chicken breast halves (about 1 pound)
1/2 package Pepperidge Farm frozen puff pastry sheets (1 sheet, 17 by 25 inches)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium skillet over medium-high heat, melt the margarine.
2. Season the chicken with salt and pepper if desired.
3. Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned. Remove the chicken from the skillet.
4. Cover and refrigerate the chicken for at least 15 minutes.
5. Thaw the puff pastry sheet at room temperature for about 30 minutes.
6. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
7. In a small bowl, mix together the egg and water; set aside.
8. Unfold the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface.
9. Roll into an 14-inch square and cut into four 7-inch squares.
10. Spread 1 teaspoon mustard onto each square.
11. Place one slice of ham, one slice of cheese, and one chicken breast half onto each square.
12. Brush the edges of the squares with the egg mixture.
13. Fold the corners to the center on top of the chicken and seal the edges.
14. Place seam-side down on a shallow-sided baking sheet.
15. Brush the tops with the egg mixture.
16. Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown. Serves 4.
